sections.title: 4. `src/lib/calculationCore.ts` — edge cases missing from the existing suite
5. `src/lib/auth.ts` — no direct tests
6. Integration/server gaps (need `fetch` mocking — not currently unit-testable)
Priority order for implementation
sections.content:
1. **`tenantScope.test.ts`** and **`safeUrl.test.ts`** — both guard against real security/privacy failures (cross-tenant leaks, XSS) and are pure functions, trivial to test.
2. **`rbac.test.ts`** — small, high-signal, catches permission-matrix typos.
3. Extend `calculationCore.test.ts` with the credit/unknown-unit/zero-expense cases above.
4. `propertyStatementAdapter.test.ts` and `paymentLedger.test.ts` (category normalization, `getResidentsCount` hardcoded lookups, balance-after-payment math) — not sketched above for brevity, but same pattern as `calculationCore`.
5. `server/payments.ts`, `server/mydata.ts` integration tests — lowest priority since they require request mocking infrastructure the project doesn't have yet (no supertest in `package.json`); worth flagging to the user before investing here.

No existing test file currently imports React components (`UsersView`, dashboard views) — there is **no component/UI test coverage at all**. If that's in scope, it needs a testing-library setup first (not present in `package.json`), which is a bigger decision than a coverage gap fix.
